rawText:
Several weeks into the military campaign against Iran, the United States finds itself trapped in a strategic bind. Washington launched its strikes expecting swift capitulation, but Tehran has refused to buckle. Despite devastating losses to its navy, air defenses, and missile infrastructure, the Iranian regime remains intact. Over two thousand civilians have been killed, yet the government continues to function and its leadership projects calm confidence. In conflicts like this, the mere survival of the weaker party can amount to a form of victory, reshaping perceptions of strength across the region.

The core dilemma centers on four courses of action, none of which offers a reliable path to ending the hostilities. Diplomacy appears nearly impossible. Trust has collapsed entirely, and the two sides' demands are so far apart that meaningful negotiation seems implausible. A second option would be to declare the mission accomplished and withdraw. However, Iran would still possess hundreds of kilograms of highly enriched uranium and retain its ability to restrict shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, the waterway through which much of the world's oil flows. For Gulf nations that have endured weeks of disruption, such an outcome would feel like betrayal. Continuing airstrikes at the current tempo represents a third possibility, but attrition alone may not force a decisive result. Iran's daily attacks have dropped significantly since the opening days, yet even reduced strikes on commercial shipping can effectively choke global energy markets.

That leaves escalation, the direction Washington appears to be leaning toward. Officials have floated threats against Iran's civilian power grid and oil export terminals, and additional military assets are moving into the region. But escalation carries enormous dangers. Tehran has warned that any attack on its energy infrastructure would trigger retaliatory strikes against power plants and desalination facilities in neighboring Gulf states. For countries that depend on desalinated water to sustain their populations, this threat is existential. Escalation may simply generate a wider cycle of violence without producing the surrender that policymakers seek.

The broader consequences of this conflict continue to multiply. Lebanon, already fragile, faces intensifying strikes in Beirut and its suburbs. The Lebanese state lacks the political will to assert authority over armed groups within its borders, creating a vacuum that external powers fill with military force. Across the region, governments watch this war with deep anxiety, aware that prolonged instability threatens their economies and social fabric. The central lesson is sobering: military superiority does not automatically translate into strategic success. When the weaker side is willing to absorb punishment and impose costs on global commerce, the stronger side may discover that every available option leads somewhere it cannot accept.

overview.title: The Iran Conflict: Four Paths, All Painful

sentences.original: Several weeks into the military campaign against Iran, the United States finds itself trapped in a strategic bind.
sentences.translation: 이란에 대한 군사 작전이 시작된 지 몇 주가 지나, 미국은 전략적 궁지에 빠져 있음을 깨닫고 있다.

grammarElements.text: Several weeks into the military campaign against Iran
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사구 (into + 명사)

grammarElements.text: itself trapped in a strategic bind
grammarElements.label: 목적격 보어 (find + O + p.p.)

vocabulary.example: The besieged city's capitulation came after weeks of bombardment.
The company found itself in a financial bind after losing its largest client.

expressions.usage: 주체가 의도치 않게 곤란한 상황에 빠져 있음을 강조하는 표현. find + 재귀대명사 + 과거분사 패턴

patterns.pattern: [Time expression] into [event], [subject] finds itself [past participle] in [predicament]
patterns.explanation: 사건이 진행된 시간을 먼저 제시하고, 주체가 곤경에 처해 있음을 발견하는 도입 구조
examples: Three months into the recession, the government found itself trapped in a policy deadlock.
Two years into the project, the team found itself mired in technical debt.

drillQuestions.question: The United States finds itself ______ in a strategic bind.

sentences.notes: 기사의 첫 문장으로 시간적 배경과 핵심 상황을 압축적으로 제시한다. find oneself + p.p. 구문은 '알고 보니 ~한 상태에 있다'는 의미로, 의도하지 않은 결과를 암시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Several weeks into the military campaign
koreanAlignment.ko: 군사 작전이 시작된 지 몇 주가 지나

sentences.original: Washington launched its strikes expecting swift capitulation, but Tehran has refused to buckle.
sentences.translation: 워싱턴은 신속한 항복을 기대하며 공습을 개시했지만, 테헤란은 굴복을 거부했다.

grammarElements.text: but Tehran has refused to buckle
grammarElements.label: but 대조절 (현재완료)

vocabulary.example: The besieged city's capitulation came after weeks of bombardment.
The regime refused to buckle under international pressure.

expressions.usage: 외부 압력에 굴하지 않고 버티는 상황을 표현. buckle은 금속이 휘어지는 이미지에서 파생된 은유

patterns.pattern: [Actor A] [past tense action] expecting [outcome], but [Actor B] has refused to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 한 쪽의 기대와 다른 쪽의 거부를 대조하여 예상이 빗나갔음을 보여주는 구조
examples: The company launched the product expecting strong demand, but consumers have refused to adopt it.
The government introduced the policy expecting compliance, but unions have refused to accept it.

drillQuestions.question: Washington launched its strikes expecting swift ______.

sentences.notes: Washington과 Tehran이라는 수도명으로 정부를 대신하는 환유(metonymy)가 사용되었다. launched(과거)와 has refused(현재완료)의 시제 대비가 '과거의 기대 vs 현재까지 지속되는 거부'를 표현한다.

sentences.original: Despite devastating losses to its navy, air defenses, and missile infrastructure, the Iranian regime remains intact.
sentences.translation: 해군, 방공 체계, 미사일 인프라에 대한 파괴적인 손실에도 불구하고, 이란 정권은 건재하다.

grammarElements.text: Despite devastating losses to its navy, air defenses, and missile infrastructure
grammarElements.label: 양보 전치사구 (삼중 병렬)

vocabulary.example: The hurricane caused devastating damage to the coastal towns.
The building's structure remained intact despite the earthquake.

expressions.usage: 파괴나 손상이 예상되는 상황에서 여전히 온전함을 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: Despite [devastating losses to A, B, and C], [subject] remains intact
patterns.explanation: 삼중 병렬로 손실을 열거한 뒤 주체가 여전히 건재함을 대조하는 양보 구조
examples: Despite devastating losses to its infrastructure, economy, and military, the government remains intact.
Despite severe damage to sales, reputation, and morale, the company remains intact.

drillQuestions.question: Despite devastating losses, the Iranian regime remains ______.

sentences.notes: Despite + 손실 목록(삼중 병렬)과 remains intact의 대조가 핵심. 군사적 피해에도 정권이 살아남는다는 역설을 한 문장으로 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: its navy, air defenses, and missile infrastructure

sentences.original: Over two thousand civilians have been killed, yet the government continues to function and its leadership projects calm confidence.
sentences.translation: 2천 명 이상의 민간인이 사망했지만, 정부는 계속 기능하고 있으며 지도부는 차분한 자신감을 투사하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: yet the government continues to function
grammarElements.label: yet 역접절

grammarElements.text: and its leadership projects calm confidence
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: The CEO projected confidence during the earnings call despite poor results.

expressions.usage: 내면의 상태와 관계없이 외부에 침착한 모습을 보여주는 것을 표현. 정치 지도자의 위기 대응에서 자주 사용

patterns.pattern: [Severe consequence], yet [subject] continues to [function] and [projects positive image]
patterns.explanation: 극심한 피해에도 불구하고 주체가 정상 작동하며 긍정적 이미지를 유지하는 대조 구조
examples: Thousands have lost their jobs, yet the company continues to operate and its management projects optimism.
The currency has lost half its value, yet the central bank continues to function and its governor projects stability.

drillQuestions.question: Its leadership ______ calm confidence.

sentences.notes: projects는 '프로젝트하다'가 아니라 '투사하다, 드러내 보이다'라는 뜻의 동사. 민간인 사상이라는 비극적 사실과 정권의 침착한 태도를 대조하는 것이 기사의 핵심 긴장감을 만든다.
pronunciationNotes: projects [prəˈdʒekts] -- 동사로 쓰일 때 두 번째 음절 강세. 명사 projects [ˈprɒdʒekts]와 구별

sentences.original: In conflicts like this, the mere survival of the weaker party can amount to a form of victory, reshaping perceptions of strength across the region.
sentences.translation: 이와 같은 분쟁에서, 약한 쪽의 단순한 생존은 하나의 승리 형태가 될 수 있으며, 역내 힘의 인식을 재편한다.

grammarElements.text: the mere survival of the weaker party
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: reshaping perceptions of strength across the region
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문

vocabulary.example: The mere mention of the scandal was enough to end his career.
The government's inaction amounts to tacit approval.

expressions.usage: 기존의 사고방식이나 판단이 근본적으로 바뀌는 것을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: In [contexts] like this, the mere [noun] of [actor] can amount to [outcome], [present participle] [broader effect]
patterns.explanation: 특정 맥락에서 단순한 사실 자체가 더 큰 의미를 갖게 되는 구조. 분사구문으로 파급 효과를 추가
examples: In negotiations like this, the mere presence of a mediator can amount to a breakthrough, shifting the dynamics of the talks.
In markets like this, the mere announcement of a policy can amount to intervention, altering investor behavior overnight.

drillQuestions.question: The mere survival of the weaker party can ______ to a form of victory.

sentences.notes: 첫 문단의 결론이자 기사 전체의 핵심 논제. 비대칭 전쟁에서 '생존 = 승리'라는 역설적 명제를 제시하며, 뒤이어 나오는 네 가지 선택지 논의의 배경을 설정한다.

koreanAlignment.en: can amount to a form of victory
koreanAlignment.ko: 하나의 승리 형태가 될 수 있다

sentences.original: The core dilemma centers on four courses of action, none of which offers a reliable path to ending the hostilities.
sentences.translation: 핵심 딜레마는 네 가지 행동 방침에 집중되는데, 그중 어느 것도 적대 행위를 종식시킬 확실한 경로를 제시하지 못한다.

grammarElements.text: none of which offers a reliable path to ending the hostilities
grammarElements.label: 계속적 관계절 (none of which)

vocabulary.example: Policymakers face a dilemma between raising taxes and cutting services.
Hostilities broke out along the border after months of tension.

expressions.usage: 앞서 언급된 복수 항목 전체를 부정할 때 사용하는 계속적 관계절 표현

patterns.pattern: The core [problem] centers on [number] [options], none of which offers [solution]
patterns.explanation: 문제의 핵심을 정의하고 모든 선택지가 불충분함을 한 문장으로 선언하는 구조
examples: The core challenge centers on three policy responses, none of which offers a painless solution.
The core dispute centers on two competing proposals, none of which satisfies all parties.

drillQuestions.question: The core dilemma centers on four courses of action, none of which offers a reliable path to ending the ______.

sentences.notes: 두 번째 문단의 주제문. none of which는 계속적 용법의 관계대명사로, 네 가지 선택지 모두를 부정하며 기사의 비관적 논조를 설정한다.

sentences.original: Diplomacy appears nearly impossible.
sentences.translation: 외교는 거의 불가능해 보인다.

vocabulary.example: Quiet diplomacy behind the scenes resolved the standoff.

expressions.usage: 단정적 표현을 피하면서도 부정적 전망을 강하게 시사하는 저널리즘적 완곡 표현

patterns.pattern: [Option] appears nearly impossible
patterns.explanation: 선택지를 짧고 단호하게 기각하는 간결한 선언 구조
examples: A peaceful resolution appears nearly impossible.
Consensus appears nearly impossible given the divisions.

drillQuestions.question: Diplomacy ______ nearly impossible.

sentences.notes: 극도로 짧은 문장으로 첫 번째 선택지(외교)를 단칼에 기각한다. 긴 문장들 사이에 배치되어 리듬적 대비를 만드는 효과가 있다.

sentences.original: Trust has collapsed entirely, and the two sides' demands are so far apart that meaningful negotiation seems implausible.
sentences.translation: 신뢰가 완전히 무너졌고, 양측의 요구가 너무 동떨어져 있어 의미 있는 협상이 불가능해 보인다.

grammarElements.text: are so far apart that meaningful negotiation seems implausible
grammarElements.label: so ... that 결과 구문

vocabulary.example: The defendant's explanation was so implausible that the jury rejected it immediately.

expressions.usage: 두 입장이 극단적으로 벌어져 있어 합의가 불가능함을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Foundation] has collapsed entirely, and [demands] are so far apart that [outcome] seems implausible
patterns.explanation: 협상의 두 가지 전제 조건(신뢰, 입장 접근)이 모두 무너졌음을 이중으로 진술하는 구조
examples: Goodwill has collapsed entirely, and the parties' positions are so far apart that compromise seems implausible.
Communication has collapsed entirely, and expectations are so far apart that alignment seems implausible.

drillQuestions.question: The two sides' demands are so far apart that meaningful negotiation seems ______.

sentences.notes: so ... that 구문은 정도가 극심하여 특정 결과가 초래됨을 나타낸다. implausible은 impossible보다 약하지만 '현실적으로 가능성이 없다'는 뉘앙스를 전달한다.

sentences.original: A second option would be to declare the mission accomplished and withdraw.
sentences.translation: 두 번째 선택지는 임무 완수를 선언하고 철수하는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: to declare the mission accomplished and withdraw
grammarElements.label: to부정사 보어 (병렬)

vocabulary.example: The president declared a state of emergency.
The troops were ordered to withdraw from the occupied territory.

expressions.usage: 실제 목표 달성과 무관하게 공식적으로 종료를 선포할 때 사용. 이라크전 'Mission Accomplished'의 역사적 울림이 있다

patterns.pattern: A [ordinal] option would be to [verb A] and [verb B]
patterns.explanation: 여러 선택지 중 하나를 가정법으로 제시하는 열거 구조
examples: A third option would be to renegotiate the terms and extend the deadline.
A first option would be to accept the loss and restructure the division.

drillQuestions.question: A second option would be to declare the mission accomplished and ______.

sentences.notes: would be는 가정법으로 '이론적 가능성'을 제시한다. mission accomplished는 2003년 이라크전에서 부시 대통령이 사용한 유명한 표현을 환기시키는 역사적 암시가 담겨 있다.

sentences.original: However, Iran would still possess hundreds of kilograms of highly enriched uranium and retain its ability to restrict shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, the waterway through which much of the world's oil flows.
sentences.translation: 그러나 이란은 여전히 수백 킬로그램의 고농축 우라늄을 보유하고, 세계 석유의 상당 부분이 지나가는 수로인 호르무즈 해협을 통한 해운을 제한하는 능력을 유지할 것이다.

grammarElements.text: hundreds of kilograms of highly enriched uranium
grammarElements.label: 목적어 1

grammarElements.text: and retain its ability to restrict shipping through the Strait of Hormuz
grammarElements.label: 가정법 동사 2 + 목적어 2

grammarElements.text: the waterway through which much of the world's oil flows
grammarElements.label: 동격 관계절

vocabulary.example: International inspectors verified the quantity of enriched uranium at the facility.
The company managed to retain most of its key employees during the downturn.

expressions.usage: 지리적 설명을 동격 관계절로 삽입하여 독자에게 배경지식을 제공하는 저널리즘 기법

patterns.pattern: However, [subject] would still [verb A] and retain its ability to [verb B]
patterns.explanation: 앞선 선택지의 결과로도 해결되지 않는 잔여 위협을 가정법으로 제시하는 반박 구조
examples: However, the company would still hold significant market share and retain its ability to undercut competitors.
However, the group would still possess operational networks and retain its ability to launch attacks.

drillQuestions.question: Iran would still possess hundreds of kilograms of highly ______ uranium.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 두 번째 선택지(철수)의 치명적 약점을 제시한다. would는 '만약 철수한다면'이라는 가정을 내포한다. 호르무즈 해협에 대한 동격 설명이 독자에게 왜 이 해협이 중요한지를 알려준다.

koreanAlignment.en: hundreds of kilograms of highly enriched uranium
retain its ability to restrict shipping
the waterway through which much of the world's oil flows
koreanAlignment.ko: 세계 석유의 상당 부분이 지나가는 수로

sentences.original: For Gulf nations that have endured weeks of disruption, such an outcome would feel like betrayal.
sentences.translation: 수주간의 혼란을 견뎌온 걸프 국가들에게, 그러한 결과는 배신처럼 느껴질 것이다.

grammarElements.text: For Gulf nations that have endured weeks of disruption
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The population endured months of rationing during the siege.
The withdrawal was seen as a betrayal by longtime allies.

expressions.usage: 동맹국의 감정적 반응을 예상하여 정책의 정치적 비용을 암시하는 표현

patterns.pattern: For [affected parties] that have endured [hardship], such an outcome would feel like [emotional response]
patterns.explanation: 피해를 감내해온 당사자의 관점에서 결과를 감정적으로 평가하는 공감 구조
examples: For workers who have endured years of wage cuts, such a deal would feel like insult.
For refugees who have endured months of displacement, such a policy would feel like abandonment.

drillQuestions.question: For Gulf nations that have endured weeks of disruption, such an outcome would feel like ______.

sentences.notes: For로 시작하여 걸프 국가들의 관점을 전경화한다. betrayal이라는 강렬한 감정적 단어가 외교적 결과의 심각성을 체감하게 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: that have endured weeks of disruption

sentences.original: Continuing airstrikes at the current tempo represents a third possibility, but attrition alone may not force a decisive result.
sentences.translation: 현재 속도로 공습을 계속하는 것이 세 번째 가능성이지만, 소모전만으로는 결정적인 결과를 강제하지 못할 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: Continuing airstrikes at the current tempo
grammarElements.label: 동명사구 주어

grammarElements.text: but attrition alone may not force a decisive result
grammarElements.label: but 대조절

vocabulary.example: The military increased the tempo of operations ahead of the deadline.
The war degenerated into a costly war of attrition.
The battle proved decisive in turning the tide of the war.

expressions.usage: alone이 주어 뒤에 오며 그것만으로는 불충분하다는 한계를 표시

patterns.pattern: [Gerund phrase] represents a [ordinal] possibility, but [X] alone may not [achieve outcome]
patterns.explanation: 선택지를 제시하면서 동시에 그 한계를 but으로 지적하는 구조
examples: Raising interest rates represents a viable option, but monetary policy alone may not control inflation.
Increasing patrols represents a practical response, but enforcement alone may not reduce crime.

drillQuestions.question: Continuing airstrikes at the current ______ represents a third possibility.

sentences.notes: 세 번째 선택지(현 수준 공습 유지)를 소개하면서 즉시 그 한계를 지적한다. tempo는 음악 용어에서 차용된 군사 용어로 '작전 속도'를 의미한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Continuing airstrikes at the current tempo
may not force a decisive result
koreanAlignment.ko: 결정적인 결과를 강제하지 못할 수 있다

sentences.original: Iran's daily attacks have dropped significantly since the opening days, yet even reduced strikes on commercial shipping can effectively choke global energy markets.
sentences.translation: 이란의 일일 공격은 개전 초기 이후 크게 감소했지만, 줄어든 상업 선박 공격조차도 글로벌 에너지 시장을 효과적으로 질식시킬 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: yet even reduced strikes on commercial shipping can effectively choke global energy markets
grammarElements.label: yet 역접절 (even 강조)

vocabulary.example: The blockade choked the country's supply lines.

expressions.phrase: even reduced strikes can effectively choke
expressions.meaning: 줄어든 공격조차도 효과적으로 질식시킬 수 있다
expressions.usage: even이 양보절의 역접을 더욱 강화하여, 규모가 줄었는데도 위협이 계속됨을 강조

patterns.pattern: [Subject]'s [metric] has dropped significantly since [start], yet even reduced [action] can effectively [severe consequence]
patterns.explanation: 수치적 개선에도 불구하고 위협이 여전히 심각함을 역접으로 보여주는 구조
examples: The company's losses have dropped significantly since the restructuring, yet even reduced deficits can effectively drain remaining reserves.
The region's violence has dropped significantly since the truce, yet even reduced incidents can effectively destabilize recovery efforts.

drillQuestions.question: Even reduced strikes on commercial shipping can effectively ______ global energy markets.

sentences.notes: choke는 '목을 조르다'는 원래 의미에서 파생된 강력한 은유로, 에너지 시장이 '숨을 못 쉰다'는 이미지를 전달한다. even의 위치가 핵심이다.

sentences.original: That leaves escalation, the direction Washington appears to be leaning toward.
sentences.translation: 그렇게 되면 남는 것은 확전, 즉 워싱턴이 기울어지고 있는 것으로 보이는 방향이다.

grammarElements.text: the direction Washington appears to be leaning toward
grammarElements.label: 동격 관계절

vocabulary.example: Both sides feared an escalation that could spiral out of control.
The board is leaning toward approving the merger.

expressions.usage: 앞선 선택지들을 모두 배제한 뒤 마지막 남은 것을 제시하는 논리적 연결 표현

patterns.pattern: That leaves [final option], the direction [actor] appears to be [verb-ing] toward
patterns.explanation: 소거법으로 마지막 선택지를 제시하고 행위자의 경향을 동격으로 덧붙이는 구조
examples: That leaves military action, the direction the alliance appears to be moving toward.
That leaves austerity, the direction the finance ministry appears to be leaning toward.

drillQuestions.question: That ______ escalation, the direction Washington appears to be leaning toward.

sentences.notes: 세 번째 문단의 도입부. That leaves는 논리적 소거법의 결론을 자연스럽게 이끌어내는 저널리즘 표현이다. appears to be leaning은 이중 완곡 표현(appears + leaning)으로 확정을 피한다.

koreanAlignment.en: the direction Washington appears to be leaning toward
koreanAlignment.ko: 워싱턴이 기울어지고 있는 것으로 보이는 방향

sentences.original: Officials have floated threats against Iran's civilian power grid and oil export terminals, and additional military assets are moving into the region.
sentences.translation: 관리들은 이란의 민간 전력망과 석유 수출 터미널에 대한 위협을 흘렸으며, 추가 군사 자산이 이 지역으로 이동하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: threats against Iran's civilian power grid and oil export terminals
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: and additional military assets are moving into the region
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (현재진행)

vocabulary.example: The minister floated the idea of a tax increase to gauge public reaction.
The navy deployed additional assets to the contested waters.

expressions.usage: 공식적으로 선언하지 않고 의도적으로 정보를 흘려 상대에게 압박을 가하는 외교/군사적 행위를 표현

patterns.pattern: [Officials] have floated [threats/proposals] against [target], and [additional actions] are [verb-ing]
patterns.explanation: 언어적 위협과 실질적 행동을 병렬로 제시하여 확전 징후를 보여주는 구조
examples: Officials have floated sanctions against the regime, and diplomatic missions are withdrawing from the capital.
Regulators have floated penalties against the industry, and enforcement teams are ramping up inspections.

drillQuestions.question: Officials have ______ threats against Iran's civilian power grid.

sentences.notes: floated는 '물 위에 띄우다'에서 파생된 은유로, 확정이 아닌 '시험 삼아 흘린다'는 뉘앙스. assets는 군사적 맥락에서 '전력, 장비'를 의미한다.

sentences.original: But escalation carries enormous dangers.
sentences.translation: 그러나 확전은 막대한 위험을 수반한다.

vocabulary.example: The project carries enormous financial risks.

expressions.usage: carry가 '수반하다, 내포하다'의 의미로 사용된 경우. 위험이 행동에 내재되어 있음을 표현

patterns.pattern: But [option] carries enormous [risks/dangers]
patterns.explanation: 짧고 단호한 문장으로 앞서 제시된 선택지의 위험성을 경고하는 전환 구조
examples: But intervention carries enormous political risks.
But deregulation carries enormous environmental dangers.

drillQuestions.question: Escalation ______ enormous dangers.

sentences.notes: 앞 문장의 확전 움직임과 극적으로 대비되는 짧은 경고 문장. carry는 '나르다'가 아니라 '수반하다'의 뜻으로, 위험이 확전이라는 행동에 본질적으로 내재되어 있음을 의미한다.

sentences.original: Tehran has warned that any attack on its energy infrastructure would trigger retaliatory strikes against power plants and desalination facilities in neighboring Gulf states.
sentences.translation: 테헤란은 자국의 에너지 인프라에 대한 어떤 공격이든 인접 걸프 국가들의 발전소와 담수화 시설에 대한 보복 공격을 촉발할 것이라고 경고했다.

grammarElements.text: that any attack on its energy infrastructure would trigger retaliatory strikes against power plants and desalination facilities in neighboring Gulf states
grammarElements.label: that절 목적어 (가정법)

vocabulary.example: The country threatened retaliatory tariffs on agricultural imports.
Desalination plants provide drinking water for much of the Gulf region.

expressions.usage: 가정법 would가 경고의 조건적 성격을 나타냄. trigger는 연쇄 반응의 시작점을 암시

patterns.pattern: [Actor] has warned that any [provocation] would trigger [retaliation] against [targets]
patterns.explanation: 경고의 내용을 that절로 제시하며, any가 모든 형태의 도발을 포괄하는 최후통첩 구조
examples: The union has warned that any further layoffs would trigger a nationwide strike against all company facilities.
Beijing has warned that any move toward independence would trigger economic retaliation against the island's key industries.

drillQuestions.question: Any attack on its energy infrastructure would trigger ______ strikes.

sentences.notes: any가 핵심. '어떤 공격이든' 모든 형태를 포괄하는 최후통첩적 경고이다. desalination facilities라는 구체적 표적 언급이 다음 문장의 '실존적 위협' 논의로 이어진다.

koreanAlignment.en: any attack on its energy infrastructure
koreanAlignment.ko: 자국의 에너지 인프라에 대한 어떤 공격이든

sentences.original: For countries that depend on desalinated water to sustain their populations, this threat is existential.
sentences.translation: 담수화된 물에 의존하여 국민의 생존을 유지하는 국가들에게, 이 위협은 실존적이다.

grammarElements.text: For countries that depend on desalinated water to sustain their populations
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Climate change poses an existential threat to island nations.
The region lacks the resources to sustain its growing population.

expressions.usage: 단순한 위험이 아니라 존재의 근본을 위협하는 수준임을 표현. 최고 수위의 경고

patterns.pattern: For [entities] that depend on [resource] to [sustain vital function], this threat is existential
patterns.explanation: 특정 자원에 생존이 달려 있는 주체에게 위협의 심각성이 극대화됨을 선언하는 구조
examples: For islands that depend on coral reefs to sustain their fisheries, this warming is existential.
For communities that depend on a single employer to sustain their economy, this closure is existential.

drillQuestions.question: For countries that depend on desalinated water, this threat is ______.

sentences.notes: existential은 철학 용어에서 파생되었지만, 현대 저널리즘에서는 '생존 자체를 위협하는'이라는 의미로 널리 사용된다. 걸프 국가들의 물 의존 문제를 한 단어로 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: For countries that depend on desalinated water

sentences.original: Escalation may simply generate a wider cycle of violence without producing the surrender that policymakers seek.
sentences.translation: 확전은 정책 입안자들이 추구하는 항복을 이끌어내지 못한 채 단지 더 넓은 폭력의 순환을 만들어낼 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: without producing the surrender that policymakers seek
grammarElements.label: without + 동명사구 (부정적 결과)

vocabulary.example: The region is trapped in a cycle of violence and retaliation.
Policymakers face difficult trade-offs between growth and inflation.

expressions.usage: 기대한 결과는 나오지 않고 부정적 결과만 나타남을 표현하는 구조

patterns.pattern: [Action] may simply generate [negative outcome] without producing [desired outcome] that [actors] seek
patterns.explanation: 어떤 행동이 원하는 결과 없이 부작용만 낳을 수 있음을 경고하는 구조
examples: Sanctions may simply generate economic hardship without producing the regime change that Western governments seek.
Regulation may simply generate compliance costs without producing the safety improvements that consumers seek.

drillQuestions.question: Escalation may simply generate a wider ______ of violence.

sentences.notes: simply가 핵심 부사로, '결국 그저 ~할 뿐'이라는 허무한 결과를 강조한다. without producing은 기대와 현실의 괴리를 명시적으로 보여주는 구조.

sentences.original: The broader consequences of this conflict continue to multiply.
sentences.translation: 이 분쟁의 더 광범위한 결과들은 계속 증가하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The broader consequences of this conflict
grammarElements.label: 주어

vocabulary.example: The scandal had broader implications for the entire industry.
Complaints continued to multiply as the service deteriorated.

expressions.usage: 단순히 늘어나는 것이 아니라 곱절로 불어나는 이미지. 통제 불능의 확산을 암시

patterns.pattern: The broader [consequences/implications] of [event] continue to [intensify]
patterns.explanation: 새 문단 도입부에서 논의 범위를 확대하며 파급 효과가 지속됨을 선언하는 구조
examples: The broader consequences of the pandemic continue to unfold.
The broader implications of the policy shift continue to emerge.

drillQuestions.question: The broader consequences of this conflict continue to ______.

sentences.notes: 마지막 문단의 도입 문장. 짧고 선언적인 구조로 논의를 전쟁 자체에서 지역적 파급 효과로 확장한다. multiply는 add(더하다)보다 훨씬 강한 증가를 암시한다.

sentences.original: Lebanon, already fragile, faces intensifying strikes in Beirut and its suburbs.
sentences.translation: 이미 취약한 레바논은 베이루트와 그 교외에서 강도가 높아지는 공습에 직면해 있다.

grammarElements.text: intensifying strikes in Beirut and its suburbs
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (현재분사 수식)

vocabulary.example: The fragile ceasefire collapsed within days.
Intensifying pressure from creditors forced the company to restructure.

expressions.usage: 삽입어로 현재 상황이 악화되기 이전에도 이미 불안정했음을 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject], already [adjective], faces [intensifying threat]
patterns.explanation: 삽입 수식어로 기존의 취약성을 알리고 새로운 위협이 더해지는 이중 위기 구조
examples: The economy, already stagnant, faces intensifying trade pressures.
The healthcare system, already strained, faces intensifying demand from an aging population.

drillQuestions.question: Lebanon, already ______, faces intensifying strikes.

sentences.notes: already fragile이라는 삽입어가 레바논의 기존 취약성을 한마디로 압축한다. 새로운 공습이 이미 불안정한 상황을 더욱 악화시킨다는 이중 구조가 핵심.

sentences.original: The Lebanese state lacks the political will to assert authority over armed groups within its borders, creating a vacuum that external powers fill with military force.
sentences.translation: 레바논 국가는 국경 내 무장 단체에 대한 권위를 주장할 정치적 의지가 부족하여, 외부 세력이 군사력으로 채우는 공백을 만들어내고 있다.

grammarElements.text: the political will to assert authority over armed groups within its borders
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + to부정사 수식

grammarElements.text: creating a vacuum that external powers fill with military force
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The new government struggled to assert its authority over the provinces.
The sudden departure of the president created a power vacuum.

expressions.phrase: creating a vacuum that ... fill with
expressions.usage: 한 쪽의 부재가 다른 세력의 개입을 초래하는 인과관계를 표현하는 구조

patterns.pattern: [Subject] lacks the [quality] to [action], creating a vacuum that [other actors] fill with [means]
patterns.explanation: 한 주체의 능력/의지 부족이 공백을 만들고 다른 세력이 그 공백을 채우는 인과 구조
examples: The UN lacks the mandate to enforce the resolution, creating a vacuum that regional powers fill with proxy forces.
The regulator lacks the resources to monitor the market, creating a vacuum that bad actors fill with fraudulent schemes.

drillQuestions.question: The Lebanese state lacks the political will to ______ authority over armed groups.

sentences.notes: vacuum(진공)은 물리학 용어에서 차용된 정치적 은유. 국가가 권위를 행사하지 못하면 그 빈 공간을 외부 세력이 채운다는 국제정치의 핵심 원리를 한 문장으로 표현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: to assert authority over armed groups
external powers fill with military force

pronunciationNotes: vacuum [ˈvækjuːm] -- '배큠'으로 첫 음절 강세, 'u'가 두 개임에 주의

sentences.original: Across the region, governments watch this war with deep anxiety, aware that prolonged instability threatens their economies and social fabric.
sentences.translation: 역내 전역에서 각국 정부는 장기적 불안정이 자국의 경제와 사회 구조를 위협한다는 것을 인식하며 이 전쟁을 깊은 불안 속에 지켜보고 있다.

grammarElements.text: aware that prolonged instability threatens their economies and social fabric
grammarElements.label: 형용사구 + that절

vocabulary.example: A prolonged recession would devastate the labor market.
The crisis threatened to tear apart the country's social fabric.

expressions.usage: 단순한 걱정이 아닌 존재적 수준의 불안을 나타내는 강화된 감정 표현

patterns.pattern: Across [scope], [actors] [verb] [event] with [emotion], aware that [consequence]
patterns.explanation: 광범위한 관찰자들의 반응과 그들이 인식하는 위협을 하나의 문장에 압축하는 파노라마적 구조
examples: Across the continent, investors watch the crisis with growing alarm, aware that contagion could spread to their own markets.
Across the industry, executives watch the regulation with unease, aware that compliance costs could erode margins.

drillQuestions.question: Governments watch this war with deep anxiety, aware that prolonged instability threatens their economies and social ______.

sentences.notes: social fabric은 '사회의 직물'이라는 은유로, 사회를 구성하는 관계와 제도의 얽힌 구조를 의미한다. aware that 이하가 불안의 구체적 이유를 설명하는 분리 형용사 구문.

sentences.original: The central lesson is sobering: military superiority does not automatically translate into strategic success.
sentences.translation: 핵심 교훈은 냉엄하다: 군사적 우위가 자동으로 전략적 성공으로 이어지지는 않는다.

grammarElements.text: military superiority does not automatically translate into strategic success
grammarElements.label: 콜론 이후 부연절

vocabulary.example: The report painted a sobering picture of the economy's future.
Air superiority proved decisive in the early stages of the campaign.
Good intentions do not always translate into effective policy.

expressions.usage: 흔히 전제되는 인과관계가 실제로는 성립하지 않음을 부정하는 표현

patterns.pattern: The central lesson is [adjective]: [power/advantage] does not automatically translate into [desired outcome]
patterns.explanation: 콜론 뒤에 핵심 명제를 선언하는 결론적 구조. '당연한 것이 당연하지 않다'는 역설을 제시
examples: The central lesson is clear: economic strength does not automatically translate into geopolitical influence.
The central lesson is humbling: technological innovation does not automatically translate into social progress.

drillQuestions.question: The central lesson is ______: military superiority does not automatically translate into strategic success.

sentences.notes: 기사의 핵심 명제를 선언하는 결론적 문장. sobering은 '술이 깨는'이라는 원래 뜻에서 파생되어 '현실을 직시하게 하는'이라는 의미. translate into는 언어 번역이 아닌 '전환, 연결'의 뜻.

sentences.original: When the weaker side is willing to absorb punishment and impose costs on global commerce, the stronger side may discover that every available option leads somewhere it cannot accept.
sentences.translation: 약한 쪽이 기꺼이 징벌을 감수하고 글로벌 교역에 비용을 부과하려 할 때, 강한 쪽은 모든 이용 가능한 선택지가 도저히 받아들일 수 없는 곳으로 이끈다는 것을 발견할 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: When the weaker side is willing to absorb punishment and impose costs on global commerce
grammarElements.label: when 조건절 (병렬 to부정사)

grammarElements.text: that every available option leads somewhere it cannot accept
grammarElements.label: that절 목적어 (접촉절 포함)

vocabulary.example: The company absorbed the losses without laying off employees.
The government imposed new tariffs on imported steel.

expressions.phrase: every available option leads somewhere it cannot accept
expressions.meaning: 모든 가능한 선택지가 받아들일 수 없는 곳으로 이끈다
expressions.usage: 완전한 전략적 교착 상태를 표현하는 절대적 부정. 관계대명사가 생략된 접촉절 구조

patterns.pattern: When [weaker actor] is willing to [endure A] and [inflict B], [stronger actor] may discover that every [option] leads somewhere [unacceptable]
patterns.explanation: 비대칭 전쟁의 역설을 조건절과 결과절로 압축하는 기사의 최종 결론 구조
examples: When the smaller firm is willing to accept short-term losses and undercut market prices, the larger rival may discover that every competitive strategy leads somewhere it cannot sustain.
When the opposition is willing to endure repression and disrupt governance, the ruling party may discover that every response leads somewhere it cannot justify.

drillQuestions.question: The weaker side is willing to ______ punishment and impose costs on global commerce.
Every available option leads somewhere it cannot ______.

sentences.notes: 기사의 마지막 문장이자 전체 논지의 압축. the weaker side / the stronger side의 대칭 구조가 비대칭 전쟁의 역설을 형식적으로도 반영한다. somewhere it cannot accept에서 관계대명사 which/that가 생략된 접촉절이 결론의 무게를 더한다.

koreanAlignment.en: When the weaker side is willing to
every available option leads somewhere it cannot accept
koreanAlignment.ko: 모든 선택지가 받아들일 수 없는 곳으로 이끈다
